ChargingStations.use16StatusNotification0: Use OCPP 1.6 Status Notifications with Connector Id 0
ChargingStations.toggleOnlineWarning: You are about to mark this charging station as
ChargingStations.toggleOnlineCaution: Warning: This action may cause the UI to be out of sync with the actual station status. Only proceed if you are certain.
ChargingStations.forceDisconnectMessage: Are you sure you want to force disconnect this charging station
ChargingStations.forceDisconnectCaution: This will immediately close the connection to the station.
ChargingStations.downloadAllMessagesToCsv: You will download all OCPP messages for charger {stationId}.
ChargingStations.downloadMessagesToCsvWithFilters: You will download OCPP messages for charger {stationId} with the following filters
ChargingStations.noDataFound: No charging station found with id {id}.

connectionModal.welcomeDescription: Watch this quick introduction video to learn how to connect charging stations. You can access this information anytime by clicking the Help button.
connectionModal.connectionDescription: Use the following tenant-specific websocket URLs to connect to the server. The connection can be upgraded from No Authentication to Security Profile 3 one by one.
connectionModal.videoNotSupported: Your browser does not support the video tag.
connectionModal.videoDescription: This video demonstrates how to connect charging stations to the CitrineOS platform.
connectionModal.step1: Configure your charging station with the appropriate websocket URL
connectionModal.step2: Choose the security profile that matches your setup
connectionModal.step3: Copy the connection URL from the connection info section
connectionModal.step4: Test the connection from your charging station
connectionModal.rememberText: You can always access this help content by clicking the Help button in the sidebar.

0.label: No Authentication
0.description: The charging station connects without credentials. Set up the username/password using this connection

1.label: Security Profile 1: Unsecured Transport with Basic Authentication
1.description: Charging Station authentication is done through a username and password. Install CA certificate in the charging station using this connection

2.label: Security Profile 2: TLS with Basic Authentication
2.description: The CSMS authenticates itself using a TLS server certificate. The Charging Stations authenticate themselves using HTTP Basic Authentication. Sign charging station certificate using this connection

3.label: Security Profile 3: TLS with Client Side Certificates
3.description: Both the Charging Station and CSMS authenticate themselves using certificates.
